parts.title: Science Teachers
prompts: Create a lesson plan for a [grade X] class in [science subject, e.g. physics] that meets the curriculum goals for [specific focus, e.g. forces], including an introduction and three activities.
Give me five strategies for motivating a [grade X] student who finds science difficult, with a simple and engaging idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class a basic experiment in [science subject, e.g. chemistry], with three steps and an example (e.g. [specific experiment]).
Suggest three ways to integrate [specific theme, e.g. sustainable development] into a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short encouraging phrases to build a [grade X] student's confidence during a science experiment.
Give me an example of a routine for starting a science lesson for a [grade X] class in [science subject], with three steps and a short investigative warm-up activity.
Suggest three ways to use everyday materials (e.g. [specific material, e.g. vinegar]) in a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with one experiment for each.
Write a short template for a 10-minute closing routine for a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with three components (e.g. reflection, summary).
Give me five ideas for making a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class more playful, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. energy], and one activity for each.
Create a list of three short reflection questions for a [grade X] class after a science lesson in [science subject] on [specific theme, e.g. ecosystems], to capture their insights.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with mathematics teachers at a [lower secondary school] to connect [science subject] to [specific theme, e.g. measurements], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple assessment of a [grade X] class's understanding in [science subject, e.g. biology], with three criteria.
Write a list of five resources (e.g. apps, videos) for a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class that support [specific goal, e.g. experiments].
Suggest three ways to adapt a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. visual impairment], with one solution for each.
Create a weekly planning template for [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. theory, experiments, reflection).
Give me five ideas for using minimal equipment in a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class to explore [specific theme, e.g. magnetism], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific concept, e.g. chemical reactions] in [science subject], with one hands-on activity for each.
Write a short guide for managing a disruptive science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with three steps and one calming strategy.
Give me an example of a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class that combines science with [specific theme, e.g. technology], with three components.
Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with PE teachers at a [primary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. body functions] in [science subject], with one example for each.
Create a lesson for a [grade X] class in [science subject] where students design a simple experiment about [specific theme, e.g. heat], with three steps and one learning goal.
Give me five ways to make a theoretical unit in [science subject] more engaging for a [grade X] class, with one creative idea for each.
Write a short guide for teaching a [grade X] class to measure [specific phenomenon, e.g. pH level] in [science subject], with three steps and one example.
Suggest three ways to connect a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class to [specific theme, e.g. climate change], with one activity for each.
Create a list of five short hands-on activities for a [grade X] class in [science subject] that strengthen [specific goal, e.g. observation].
Give me an example of a routine for closing a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class with a short discussion, with three steps.
Suggest three ways to introduce a [grade X] class to [specific scientific concept, e.g. photosynthesis] in [science subject], with one activity for each.
Suggest three ways to use digital tools (e.g. [specific tool, e.g. simulation apps]) in a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with one task for each.
Write a short template for a 5-minute reflection after a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with three questions about their learning.
Give me five ideas for incorporating games into a science lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with a focus on [specific goal, e.g. circuits], and one game for each.
Create a list of three short ways to support a [grade X] student who is struggling with [specific concept, e.g. density] in [science subject], with one example for each.
Suggest three ways to collaborate with English teachers at a [lower secondary school] to connect [science subject] to [specific theme, e.g. scientific vocabulary], with one idea for each.
Give me an example of a simple plan for teaching a [grade X] class to conduct a field experiment in [science subject] during one lesson, with three steps.
Write a list of five simple experiments for a [grade X] class in [science subject] that reinforce [specific goal, e.g. chemical processes], with a short rationale for each.
Suggest three ways to manage a [grade X] class that is reluctant to participate in a science lesson in [science subject], with one method for each.
Create a term planning template for [science subject] for a [grade X] class, with three focus areas (e.g. theory, practical work, sustainability) and time frames.
Give me five ideas for creating a simple science exhibition in [science subject] with a [grade X] class, with minimal preparation, and one component for each.
Write a short guide for supporting a [grade X] student with [specific need, e.g. language difficulties] in a science lesson in [science subject], with three steps.
Give me an example of a lesson in [science subject] for a [grade X] class that combines science with [specific theme, e.g. space], with three components.

Create a list of three short ways to collaborate with the student council at a [lower secondary school] to reinforce [specific goal, e.g. environmental awareness] in [science subject], with one example for each.
